zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Burgh於1228年出生在愛爾蘭康瑙特時，她的父親Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught35歲，母親Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught28歲。她於1240年與Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1276年在愛爾蘭享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de Burgh于1228年出生在爱尔兰康瑙特时，她的父亲Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught35岁，母亲Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught28岁。她于1240年与Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1276年在爱尔兰享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Burgh was born in 1228, in Connaught, Ireland, her father, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, was 35 and her mother, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, was 28. She married Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy in 1240, in Cork, County Cork, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1276, in Ireland, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Burgh est née en 1228, à Connaught, Irlande, son père, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy en 1240, à Cork, County Cork, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1276, à Irlande, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de Burgh 1228 in Connaught, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught 35 und ihre Mutter Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught 28. Sie hat 1240 in Cork, County Cork, Irland Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1276 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Burgh è nata il nel 1228 nel Connaught, Irlanda, sua padre, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy nel 1240, nel Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1276, nel Irlanda, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Burghが1228年にアイルランドコナウトで生まれた時，父Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaughtは35歳，母Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaughtは28歳であった。1240年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクコルクでSir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1276年にアイルランドで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Burgh(이)가 1228년 아일랜드 콘오트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1240년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에서 Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1276년 아일랜드에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Burgh nasceu em 1228, em Connaught, Irlanda, seu pai, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy em 1240, em Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1276, em Irlanda, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Burgh родилась в 1228 г. в местности коннот, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy в 1240 г. в местности корк, коунти корк, Ирландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1276 г. в местности Ирландия в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda de Burgh nació en 1228, en Connaught, Irlanda, su papá, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy en 1240, en Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1276, en Irlanda, a la edad de 48 años.
